title: Spring Boot Backend Developer Assessment
description: Practical evaluation focusing on Spring Boot development, REST API implementation, Spring Data JPA, validation, exception handling, unit/integration testing, logging, and common backend development patterns.

questions.text: Which dependency is required to build a REST API with Spring Boot?
questions.explanation: spring-boot-starter-web includes Spring MVC, Tomcat, and REST support. This is the essential starter for building web/REST backends.

questions.text: What is the correct way to create a REST endpoint that returns JSON in Spring Boot?
questions.explanation: @RestController is the preferred way for REST APIs as it automatically applies @ResponseBody to all handler methods, converting return objects to JSON.

questions.text: How do you inject a dependency into a Spring Boot service?
options: Using @Autowired on constructor, field, or setter
Using dependency injection is not possible in Spring Boot
questions.explanation: @Autowired tells Spring to inject the dependency. Constructor injection is recommended as it makes dependencies explicit and helps with unit testing.

questions.text: Which annotation maps HTTP POST requests to a controller method?
questions.explanation: @PostMapping is the specialized annotation for POST requests. @RequestMapping with method=POST also works but @PostMapping is cleaner.

questions.text: How do you extract a query parameter like '?page=2' in a Spring Boot controller?
questions.explanation: @RequestParam binds query parameters. Example: @GetMapping("/users") public List<User> getUsers(@RequestParam int page).

questions.text: What is the purpose of @Entity annotation in Spring Boot JPA?
options: It creates a database table automatically
It marks a class as a JPA entity to be mapped to a database table
It creates an index on the primary key
questions.explanation: @Entity marks a POJO as a JPA entity. Combined with @Id and optionally @Table, it defines the mapping to a database table.

questions.text: Which interface should your repository extend to get basic CRUD methods for an entity?
questions.explanation: Extending CrudRepository (or JpaRepository) gives you methods like save(), findById(), findAll(), delete(), etc., without writing implementations.

questions.text: What does the @RequestBody annotation do in a controller method?
options: It reads data from URL query parameters
It binds the HTTP request body to a method parameter (e.g., JSON to Java object)
questions.explanation: @RequestBody tells Spring to deserialize the HTTP request body (usually JSON) into a Java object using HttpMessageConverter.

questions.text: What is the default HTTP response status code for a successful GET request in Spring Boot?
questions.explanation: Spring Boot returns 200 OK by default for successful GET requests. You can customize with @ResponseStatus if needed.

questions.text: How do you change the server port in Spring Boot?
options: Change the port in the Java code
questions.explanation: Adding server.port=8081 (or any port) to application.properties overrides the default 8080. Environment variables can also override it.

questions.text: Which annotation is used to validate Java beans (e.g., checking @NotNull, @Size) in Spring Boot?
questions.explanation: @Valid (from javax.validation) triggers validation on a method parameter. Combined with @NotNull, @Size, @Email annotations on fields.

questions.text: What does the @ExceptionHandler annotation do?
options: It defines a method to handle specific exceptions within a controller
questions.explanation: @ExceptionHandler inside a controller handles exceptions thrown by handler methods in that controller. For global handling, use @ControllerAdvice.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the spring-boot-starter-test dependency?
options: To provide JUnit, Mockito, AssertJ, and other testing libraries
questions.explanation: spring-boot-starter-test brings in JUnit Jupiter, Mockito, AssertJ, Hamcrest, and Spring Test utilities for comprehensive testing.

questions.text: How do you write a simple unit test for a Spring Boot service using Mockito?
options: Use @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) and @Mock for dependencies, then @InjectMocks for the service
questions.explanation: For unit tests, @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) with @Mock and @InjectMocks is sufficient. @SpringBootTest is for integration tests.

questions.text: Which annotation is used to run SQL scripts before integration tests?
questions.explanation: @Sql (org.springframework.test.context.jdbc.Sql) executes SQL scripts before or after test methods to set up test data.

questions.text: What does the @Transactional annotation do in a service method?
options: It ensures the method runs within a database transaction; any RuntimeException triggers rollback
questions.explanation: @Transactional wraps the method in a transaction. If a runtime exception occurs, Spring rolls back changes; otherwise commits on normal completion.

questions.text: What is the purpose of Spring Boot Actuator?
options: To provide production-ready monitoring endpoints like /health, /info, /metrics
questions.explanation: Actuator adds endpoints for health checks, metrics, environment info, and more. Essential for monitoring Spring Boot apps in production.

questions.text: How do you enable logging for a specific package in Spring Boot?
questions.explanation: Spring Boot uses Logback by default. Configure logging levels per package via logging.level.package.name=DEBUG in application.properties.

questions.text: Which annotation simplifies using Lombok to generate getters, setters, toString, equals, and hashCode?
questions.explanation: @Data is a Lombok annotation that generates getters, setters, toString, equals, hashCode, and a required args constructor.

questions.text: What is the correct way to define a one-to-many relationship in JPA?
options: @OneToMany on the parent side, @ManyToOne on the child side
questions.explanation: In a bidirectional one-to-many, the parent uses @OneToMany(mappedBy="parent") and the child uses @ManyToOne with @JoinColumn.

questions.text: How do you create a custom query in Spring Data JPA repository?
options: Using @Query annotation with JPQL or native SQL
Writing SQL in a separate file
questions.explanation: @Query allows you to write custom JPQL or native SQL queries directly in the repository interface. Example: @Query("SELECT u FROM User u WHERE u.email = ?1").

questions.text: What does the @Value annotation do in Spring Boot?
options: It sets the value of a primitive variable
It injects values from properties files into fields (e.g., @Value("${app.name}"))
It creates a new instance of a class
questions.explanation: @Value reads configuration from application.properties or environment variables and injects them into Spring-managed beans.

questions.text: What is the purpose of spring-boot-devtools?
options: To enable automatic application restarts during development
questions.explanation: DevTools provides automatic restarts when files change in development, improving productivity. Disabled automatically in production.

questions.text: Which HTTP status code should you return for a successful POST request that creates a new resource?
questions.explanation: 201 Created is the standard response for POST that creates a resource. Include a Location header pointing to the new resource.

questions.text: How do you make a field optional in a Spring Boot @RequestParam?
options: Both of the above are valid approaches
questions.explanation: You can set required=false or use java.util.Optional as the parameter type. Both make the parameter optional. Default value can be set with defaultValue attribute.
